name: Jabir ibn Hajjaj
title: Soldier of Insight
shortDescription: A brave Shia of Kufa from the Bani Taym clan who was martyred defending the Imam in the mass attack on the morning of Ashura.
fullStory:
Jabir ibn Hajjaj al-Taymi was a brave warrior and sincere Shia in the city of Kufa. He was from the Bani Taym clan and was always known among the people as an insightful individual and supporter of truth.

When Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan was surrounded by Ibn Ziyad's armies, Jabir could not stay in Kufa. He bravely escaped the city and reached Karbala to sacrifice his life in the path of defending Wilayah.

On the morning of Ashura, with Umar Sa'd's order to attack, the Kufan army cowardly launched a mass assault on the Imam's tents. Jabir ibn Hajjaj was one of the warriors who stood in the front line of defense.

He fought with great skill in hand-to-hand combat and against enemy archers. In this fierce attack where many of the Imam's loyal companions were martyred, Jabir also fought valiantly.

Finally, after an earnest resistance, Jabir ibn Hajjaj fell to the ground from the cowardly blows of the enemy and attained the great grace of martyrdom. His name was recorded in history as one of the martyrs of the first attack.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى جابِرِ بْنِ حَجّاجٍ التَّيْمِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Jabir ibn Hajjaj al-Taymi.
